papers.title_en: Generative Modeling on Metric Graphs via Neural Optimal Transport
papers.authors: Alessandro Micheli, Yueqi Cao, Anthea Monod, Samir Bhatt
papers.evidence: 度量图上源和目标测度之间的神经最优传输
papers.tldr: 度量图上连续概率分布的生成建模面临挑战，现有方法多限于离散分布或图结构生成。本文提出首个基于神经最优传输的深度生成框架，通过将图嵌入光滑空间（欧氏或热带Abel-Jacobi），用神经半对偶参数化求解熵Kantorovich问题，再投影回原图。在多种图结构上，该方法匹配或优于离散图OT基线，且扩展性更优。在纽约曼哈顿百万级Uber上车点数据上验证了实用性。该工作首次实现度量图连续分布可扩展生成，并提供弱收敛理论保证。
papers.abstract_en: We introduce, to our knowledge, the first deep generative modeling framework for probability distributions continuously supported on compact metric graphs. Given source and target measures on a metric graph, our method embeds the graph into a smooth ambient space, solves an entropic Kantorovich problem via a neural semidual parameterization, and projects generated samples back onto the original graph. We study two embedded geometries: an extrinsic Euclidean realization and the intrinsic tropical Abel--Jacobi embedding into the Jacobian torus. In both cases, the resulting generator is graph-supported by construction. We prove that, in the joint limit of increasing neural expressivity, the learned generator converges weakly to a valid transport coupling between the original graph measures. Empirically, across a range of geometrically distinct graphs, our method matches or improves upon heuristic transport baselines based on discrete graph OT, while scaling more favorably. Finally, we demonstrate scalability on real-world urban mobility data by training our model on one million Uber pickup locations in Manhattan, New York City.
